title: README: document fish setup
description:
## Parent

dotfiles-wq3 — Migrate shell configuration from zsh to fish

## What to build

Update the dotfiles README with everything needed to reproduce the fish setup on a new machine: prerequisites to install, the symlink command, and how to try fish without committing to it as the default shell.

End-to-end behavior: after this slice, someone setting up dotfiles on a fresh Mac can follow the README to get fish working in under 5 minutes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] README documents brew install fnm direnv as prerequisites
- [ ] README documents the symlink command: ln -s ~/dotfiles/.config/fish ~/.config/fish
- [ ] README explains that fish_variables and config.local.fish are gitignored
- [ ] README explains how to try fish (run fish from zsh) vs make it default (chsh)
- [ ] README notes that fisher is installed interactively, not via symlink

## Blocked by

- dotfiles-wq3.1 — Scaffold fish config structure and symlink
- dotfiles-wq3.2 — Core environment: PATH, env vars, safe aliases, eza
- dotfiles-wq3.3 — Prompt and navigation: Starship, zoxide, fzf
- dotfiles-wq3.4 — fnm: Node version manager
- dotfiles-wq3.5 — direnv: scoped environment loading
- dotfiles-wq3.6 — fisher: plugin manager

title: fisher: plugin manager
description:
## Parent

dotfiles-wq3 — Migrate shell configuration from zsh to fish

## What to build

Install fisher (fish's plugin manager) and document how to use it for future plugin installs. No plugins are installed in this slice — fisher is set up as infrastructure only.

End-to-end behavior: after this slice, running fisher install <plugin> in fish works. The fisher install command itself is a one-time interactive step, not something sourced in config.fish.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] fisher is installed and available in fish
- [ ] fisher list runs without error
- [ ] A comment or README note explains how to install plugins in the future (fisher install owner/repo)

## Blocked by

- dotfiles-wq3.1 — Scaffold fish config structure and symlink

title: fnm: Node version manager
description:
## Parent

dotfiles-wq3 — Migrate shell configuration from zsh to fish

## What to build

Add fnm (Fast Node Manager) integration to config.fish. fnm replaces nvm for fish — it reads existing .nvmrc files and auto-switches Node versions on directory entry.

End-to-end behavior: after this slice, entering a directory with a .nvmrc file automatically switches to the specified Node version. Prerequisite: brew install fnm must be run before this config is active.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] fnm env --use-on-cd | source is in config.fish
- [ ] Entering a directory with a .nvmrc switches Node version automatically
- [ ] brew install fnm is documented as a prerequisite (in a comment in config.fish or in the README)

## Blocked by

- dotfiles-wq3.1 — Scaffold fish config structure and symlink

title: direnv: scoped environment loading
description:
## Parent

dotfiles-wq3 — Migrate shell configuration from zsh to fish

## What to build

Add direnv integration to config.fish. direnv replaces the oh-my-zsh dotenv plugin and solves its core problem: environment variables are strictly scoped to the directory they're defined in and automatically unloaded on exit.

End-to-end behavior: after this slice, adding a .envrc to a project directory and running direnv allow loads its variables on cd-in and unloads them on cd-out. Prerequisite: brew install direnv must be run before this config is active.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] direnv hook fish | source is in config.fish
- [ ] Variables defined in a .envrc are available inside the directory
- [ ] Variables are unset after leaving the directory
- [ ] brew install direnv is documented as a prerequisite

## Blocked by

- dotfiles-wq3.1 — Scaffold fish config structure and symlink

title: Prompt and navigation: Starship, zoxide, fzf
description:
## Parent

dotfiles-wq3 — Migrate shell configuration from zsh to fish

## What to build

Wire up the three navigation and prompt tools into config.fish: Starship for the prompt, zoxide as the cd replacement, and fzf for fuzzy search integrations.

End-to-end behavior: after this slice, the fish prompt renders via Starship (no-op in Warp), cd uses zoxide so partial directory names work, and Ctrl+R triggers fzf fuzzy history search.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] starship init fish | source is in config.fish
- [ ] zoxide init fish --cmd cd | source is in config.fish (cd is zoxide, not builtin)
- [ ] fzf --fish | source is in config.fish
- [ ] z partial-name navigates to a frecent directory
- [ ] Ctrl+R opens fzf history search

## Blocked by

- dotfiles-wq3.1 — Scaffold fish config structure and symlink

title: Core environment: PATH, env vars, safe aliases, eza
description:
## Parent

dotfiles-wq3 — Migrate shell configuration from zsh to fish

## What to build

Populate config.fish with all foundational environment setup: PATH entries, GPG_TTY, JAVA_HOME, XDG variables, interactive-safe aliases for rm/cp/mv, eza aliases for ls and ll, and conditional sourcing of config.local.fish at the end.

End-to-end behavior: after this slice, opening fish gives the correct PATH, custom scripts in ~/bin and ~/dotfiles/bin are available, eza is used for ls/ll with the same flags as zsh, and machine-specific overrides can be dropped in config.local.fish without touching the repo.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] PATH includes ~/bin and ~/dotfiles/bin
- [ ] GPG_TTY is set via (tty)
- [ ] JAVA_HOME is set via /usr/libexec/java_home
- [ ] XDG_CONFIG_HOME, XDG_DATA_HOME, XDG_STATE_HOME, XDG_CACHE_HOME are set
- [ ] rm, cp, mv are aliased with -i flag
- [ ] ls uses eza -G --color auto --icons -a
- [ ] ll uses eza -l --color always --icons -a -snew
- [ ] config.local.fish is sourced at the end if it exists
- [ ] All variables use fish set -gx syntax (no export statements)

## Blocked by

- dotfiles-wq3.1 — Scaffold fish config structure and symlink

title: Scaffold fish config structure and symlink
description:
## Parent

dotfiles-wq3 — Migrate shell configuration from zsh to fish

## What to build

Create the fish configuration directory structure inside the dotfiles repo and replace the existing ~/.config/fish directory with a symlink into dotfiles. This is the foundation all other fish slices depend on.

End-to-end behavior: after this slice, fish reads its config from ~/dotfiles/.config/fish/, the directory is version-controlled, and runtime-generated files don't pollute git.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ~/dotfiles/.config/fish/ exists with subdirectories: conf.d/, functions/, completions/
- [ ] ~/dotfiles/.config/fish/config.fish exists (minimal skeleton with interactive guard)
- [ ] ~/.config/fish is a symlink pointing to ~/dotfiles/.config/fish/
- [ ] fish_variables is listed in ~/dotfiles/.gitignore
- [ ] config.local.fish is listed in ~/dotfiles/.gitignore
- [ ] git status shows no untracked or dirty files from the fish config directory

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately

title: Migrate shell configuration from zsh to fish
description:
## Problem Statement

The user's current shell configuration is built on zsh with oh-my-zsh, zinit, and a collection of plugins. This setup has accumulated complexity over time (multiple plugin managers, commented-out dead config, slow startup). The user wants to evaluate fish as a daily shell driver and needs a modern, clean fish configuration that preserves their existing workflow with minimal friction.

## Solution

Create a fish configuration in the dotfiles repo (~/dotfiles/.config/fish/) that mirrors the user's zsh workflow: same eza aliases, same zoxide/fzf integrations, same Starship prompt, same local-override pattern. Replace zsh-specific tools with fish-native equivalents where possible (nvm → fnm, dotenv plugin → direnv). Symlink ~/.config/fish → ~/dotfiles/.config/fish to keep everything version-controlled.

## User Stories

1. As a shell user, I want fish autosuggestions as I type, so that I can accept common commands without retyping them.
2. As a shell user, I want syntax highlighting as I type commands, so that I can spot typos before pressing Enter.
3. As a shell user, I want my eza aliases (ls, ll) to work exactly as in zsh, so that my muscle memory is undisturbed.
4. As a shell user, I want zoxide to handle cd, so that I can jump to frequently-visited directories with partial names.
5. As a shell user, I want fzf integrations available in fish, so that I can use fuzzy history search and file finding.
6. As a shell user, I want Starship to initialize in fish, so that my prompt looks the same as in zsh (and is a no-op in Warp).
7. As a shell user, I want fnm to manage my Node.js versions, so that my existing .nvmrc files continue to work.
8. As a shell user, I want direnv to scope .env loading strictly to a directory and its children, so that environment variables don't leak across projects.
9. As a shell user, I want a config.local.fish that is sourced last, so that I can keep machine-specific settings out of the repo.
10. As a shell user, I want fisher available, so that I can install fish plugins in the future without extra setup.
11. As a shell user, I want rm, cp, mv to default to interactive mode (-i), so that I don't accidentally overwrite files.
12. As a shell user, I want my PATH set correctly (~/bin, ~/dotfiles/bin, Homebrew), so that all my custom scripts and tools are available.
13. As a shell user, I want GPG_TTY set correctly, so that GPG signing works in fish.
14. As a shell user, I want JAVA_HOME set via /usr/libexec/java_home, so that Java tools work correctly.
15. As a shell user, I want the fish config managed in my dotfiles repo and symlinked to ~/.config/fish, so that it is version-controlled alongside my other configs.
16. As a shell user, I want fish_variables gitignored, so that runtime universal variable state doesn't pollute my dotfiles commits.
17. As a dotfiles maintainer, I want the README updated with the symlink instruction for fish, so that I can reproduce the setup on a new machine.

## Implementation Decisions

- **Config location**: ~/dotfiles/.config/fish/ is the source of truth. ~/.config/fish is a symlink to it. The existing ~/.config/fish directory must be cleared first (it contains only a skeleton config.fish).
- **Directory structure**: config.fish (main entry), conf.d/ (auto-loaded fragments), functions/ (fish functions), completions/ (tab completions).
- **fish_variables**: Gitignored — it is written by fish at runtime and contains no user-authored config.
- **Plugin manager**: fisher installed via the official one-line curl command. No plugins installed initially — just the manager.
- **Node version manager**: fnm (brew install fnm). Replaces nvm. Reads existing .nvmrc files transparently. fnm env --use-on-cd enables auto-switching.
- **direnv**: brew install direnv. fish hook added to config.fish. Replaces oh-my-zsh dotenv plugin. Requires explicit `direnv allow` per directory.
- **Prompt**: starship init fish | source. Existing ~/.config/starship.toml is reused unchanged.
- **zoxide**: zoxide init fish --cmd cd | source. Same --cmd cd flag as current zsh config.
- **fzf**: fzf --fish | source (fzf 0.48+ syntax for fish).
- **eza aliases**: Defined as fish abbreviations or aliases — same flags as zsh (ls: -G --color auto --icons -a, ll: -l --color always --icons -a -snew).
- **Safe aliases**: rm/cp/mv with -i flag.
- **Environment variables**: PATH, GPG_TTY, JAVA_HOME, XDG_* vars set in config.fish (fish equivalent of .zshenv).
- **Local overrides**: config.fish sources ~/.config/fish/config.local.fish if it exists. This file is gitignored.
- **Autosuggestions + syntax highlighting**: Built into fish — no plugin needed.
- **forgit**: Removed. Not actively used.
- **oh-my-zsh dotenv plugin**: Removed. Replaced by direnv.
- **zsh-specific tools removed**: zinit, oh-my-zsh, zsh-autosuggestions plugin, zsh-syntax-highlighting plugin, nvm.

## Testing Decisions

Fish config is not unit-testable in a meaningful way. Verification is manual:
- Launch fish, confirm Starship prompt renders.
- Confirm ls and ll use eza.
- Confirm cd uses zoxide (z partial-name works).
- Confirm fzf Ctrl+R history search works.
- Create a .nvmrc file, cd into the directory, confirm fnm auto-switches.
- Create a .envrc with direnv allow, confirm variable is set; cd out, confirm it unsets.
- Confirm config.local.fish is sourced if present.
- Confirm fish_variables is not tracked by git.

## Out of Scope

- Migrating zsh config to a parallel clean state (zsh is left untouched).
- Setting fish as the default shell (chsh) — user can do this manually when ready.
- Migrating any zsh-specific scripting in bin/ to fish syntax.
- direnv .envrc files for existing projects — user sets those up per-project.
- Any prompt customization (Starship config is unchanged).

## Further Notes

- fish 4.7.1 is already installed via Homebrew.
- fnm and direnv are not yet installed — brew install fnm direnv needed as a prerequisite step.
- The existing ~/.config/fish directory has only a skeleton config.fish — safe to replace with a symlink after copying nothing of value.
- fish uses a different config language (not bash-compatible). Aliases are `alias name 'command'` or abbreviations. There are no `export` statements — use `set -gx VAR value`.
